logp_all.csv.description: Merged and deduplicated experimental LogP (octanol-water partition coefficient) dataset from multiple public sources. Standardized using the ChEMBL pipeline (salt removal, charge neutralization, tautomer canonicalization). Multi-component mixtures dropped; known pharmaceutical counterions stripped. For compounds present in SangsterLogP (Cirino et al. 2026 — the most rigorously curated source), the `logp` value is taken from SangsterLogP; otherwise it is the cross-source mean (see `primary_source` column). Joinable on `smiles` to logd_all.csv.

columns.smiles: Standardized canonical SMILES (RDKit, neutralized, canonical tautomer)
columns.logp: Experimental logP — Sangster value if present, else cross-source mean
columns.logp_std: Standard deviation of logP across all reporting sources (0 if single source)
columns.logp_count: Number of sources reporting this compound
columns.sources: Pipe-delimited list of source names that reported this compound
columns.primary_source: Which source the `logp` value was taken from: 'sangster' or 'consensus' (mean across non-Sangster sources)
columns.logp_values: Pipe-delimited individual logP values from each source

logp_sangster.csv.description: SangsterLogP experimental LogP dataset (~23,500 compounds), the largest publicly curated logP collection. Originates from Dr. James Sangster's review of >3,000 published sources, curated via the OCHEM platform with systematic logD-to-logP correction for ionisable compounds (ChemAxon pKa + ΔDP shift) and consensus-based outlier/duplicate removal. Spans -3.8 to 11.7 log units and includes ~800 beyond-Rule-of-5 (MW > 700) compounds, substantially expanding chemical space coverage vs PHYSPROP. Distributed under CC-BY-4.0 via Zenodo. The `logp` column here is the experimental-or-adjusted value (logD measurements corrected to logP via ΔDP) from the main sheet of the source XLSX.

columns.logp: Experimental or adjusted logP (octanol-water partition coefficient)

references: https://doi.org/10.1038/s41597-026-07357-2 (Cirino, Caron, Ermondi, Charochkina, Tetko; SangsterLogP: the largest publicly available dataset of logP values, Sci Data 2026)
Sangster, J. Octanol-Water Partition Coefficients of Simple Organic Compounds. J. Phys. Chem. Ref. Data 18, 1111-1229 (1989). https://doi.org/10.1063/1.555833

logp_opera_physprop.csv.description: PHYSPROP/OPERA experimental LogP training data (~4,200 compounds). Curated by the EPA from the PHYSPROP database, originally used to train the KOWWIN and OPERA LogP models. Extracted from OPERA_Data.zip in the NIEHS/OPERA GitHub repository.
references: https://doi.org/10.1186/s13321-018-0263-1 (Mansouri et al., OPERA models, J Cheminform 2018)

logp_graphormer_logp.csv.description: GraphormerLogP (GLP) experimental LogP dataset (~42,000 compounds). Multi-source curation by the Chemoinformatics and Molecular Modeling Laboratory (CIMM) at Kazan Federal University. Aggregated from OpenChem, Huuskonen, SAMPL6/7, and other published datasets.
references: https://doi.org/10.1021/acs.jcim.4c00654 (Safin et al., GraphormerLogP, J Chem Inf Model 2024)

logd_all.csv.description: Merged and deduplicated experimental LogD (octanol-water distribution coefficient at pH 7.4) dataset. Standardized using the ChEMBL pipeline so SMILES are joinable to logp_all.csv. Currently a single source; structured for additional sources to be merged in.

columns.smiles: Standardized canonical SMILES (RDKit, neutralized, canonical tautomer)
columns.logd: Mean experimental logD at pH 7.4 across sources
columns.logd_std: Standard deviation of logD across sources (0 if single source)
columns.logd_count: Number of sources reporting this compound
columns.logd_values: Pipe-delimited individual logD values from each source

references: https://moleculenet.org/datasets-1 (AstraZeneca logD via ChEMBL, MoleculeNet mirror)

logd_astrazeneca_chembl.csv.description: AstraZeneca experimental logD measurements at pH 7.4 (~4,200 compounds), originally published in ChEMBL. Fetched directly from the MoleculeNet S3 mirror; same data is also redistributed by DeepChem and Therapeutic Data Commons (as `Lipophilicity_AstraZeneca`).

columns.logd: Experimental logD at pH 7.4 (octanol-water distribution coefficient)

logp_logd_overlap_07_10.csv.description: Multi-task LogD/LogP dataset, HIGH-overlap tier. All 4,199 LogD compounds plus LogP rows whose best-Tanimoto-to-LogD falls in [0.7, 1.0]. The band includes Tanimoto = 1.0 (exact-SMILES matches), so a portion of rows have both `logp` and `logd` populated — these are the same compounds measured in both source datasets. Auxiliary chemistry is close to LogD's; this tier represents the 'duplicate / nearby supervision' mode. Built by data/public_data/build_logp_logd_overlap.py.

columns.smiles: Standardized canonical SMILES (joinable to logp_all.csv and logd_all.csv)
columns.logd: Experimental logD at pH 7.4 (NaN if not measured)

logp_logd_overlap_03_07.csv.description: Multi-task LogD/LogP dataset, MEDIUM-overlap tier. All 4,199 LogD compounds plus LogP rows whose best-Tanimoto-to-LogD falls in [0.3, 0.7). The band excludes Tanimoto = 1.0, so the auxiliary LogP rows share no SMILES with LogD — no rows have both columns populated. Auxiliary chemistry is similar to LogD's neighborhood without duplicating it; this tier represents the 'complementary supervision' mode. Built by data/public_data/build_logp_logd_overlap.py.

columns.smiles: Standardized canonical SMILES (joinable to logp_all.csv and logd_all.csv)
columns.logd: Experimental logD at pH 7.4 (NaN if not measured)

logp_logd_overlap_00_03.csv.description: Multi-task LogD/LogP dataset, LOW-overlap tier. All 4,199 LogD compounds plus LogP rows whose best-Tanimoto-to-LogD falls in [0.0, 0.3) — auxiliary chemistry the LogD set doesn't cover. Tests whether 'novel' auxiliary representation transfers to LogD prediction. Auxiliary LogP rows share no SMILES with LogD. Built by data/public_data/build_logp_logd_overlap.py.

columns.smiles: Standardized canonical SMILES (joinable to logp_all.csv and logd_all.csv)
columns.logd: Experimental logD at pH 7.4 (NaN if not measured)

tagging.csv.description: Reference compounds with known expected outputs of workbench.utils.chem_utils.mol_tagging.tag_molecules(). Each row pairs an input SMILES with the standardized SMILES, undefined-chiral count, and the curation-namespace tags that should be emitted after running standardize() then tag_molecules(). Complements the standardization reference set: that set exercises salt / charge / tautomer / stereo handling; this set exercises ADMET-curation tag derivation across composition, structure, physchem, liability, and curation categories. Used as a regression gate — changes that shift any expected tag are either intentional policy updates or bugs.

columns.name: Short identifier for the test case
columns.input_smiles: Raw SMILES fed to standardize() then tag_molecules()
columns.expected_undefined_chiral_centers: Expected value of the 'undefined_chiral_centers' column from standardize() — count of chiral centers in the original input SMILES with no stereo flag
columns.expected_curation_tags: Pipe-delimited list of curation:* tags expected from tag_molecules() (empty string means no curation flags expected)
columns.notes: Why this compound is in the reference set

pxr_train.csv.description: Primary training set for the OpenADMET PXR Induction Blind Challenge (activity track). Dose-response pEC50 and Emax for human Pregnane-X Receptor (hPXR) induction, measured with OpenADMET's in-house high-fidelity cell-based assay. PXR is the master regulator of CYP3A4 (which metabolises ~50% of marketed drugs), so PXR induction is a primary driver of drug-drug interaction liability.

columns.ocnt_batch: Octant batch identifier for the assayed material
columns.pec50: Dose-response potency, -log10(EC50 in M) — the challenge prediction target
columns.emax_estimate: Maximal induction efficacy (Emax) from the dose-response fit
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate: Emax expressed relative to the positive-control agonist
columns.pec50_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for pec50
columns.pec50_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for pec50
columns.emax_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for emax_estimate
columns.emax_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for emax_estimate
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate

pxr_test_blinded.csv.description: Blinded activity-track test set (513 compounds) for the PXR challenge — SMILES only, ground-truth pEC50 withheld for scoring (Relative Absolute Error). Split into two analog sets: Analog Set 1 (scored on the Phase 1 live leaderboard, then unblinded on 2026-05-26 — see pxr_test_phase1_unblinded) and Analog Set 2 (held out for final scoring after 2026-07-01).

pxr_test_phase1_unblinded.csv.description: Analog Set 1 of the activity-track test set with ground-truth pEC50 revealed at the Phase 1 to Phase 2 transition (2026-05-26). Serves as a held-out evaluation set and as additional training signal for Phase 2 submissions targeting Analog Set 2.

columns.ocnt_batch: Octant batch identifier for the assayed material
columns.pec50_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for pec50
columns.pec50_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for pec50
columns.emax_estimate: Maximal induction efficacy (Emax) from the dose-response fit
columns.emax_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for emax_estimate
columns.emax_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for emax_estimate
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate: Emax expressed relative to the positive-control agonist
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate
columns.phase: Challenge phase the compound belongs to

pxr_counter_assay_train.csv.description: PXR-null counter-assay training data. Reports the null-line (non-PXR / off-target) response used to flag and discount assay artifacts, so apparent PXR activity can be separated from non-specific signal.

columns.ocnt_batch: Octant batch identifier for the assayed material
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate: Counter-assay Emax relative to the positive-control agonist
columns.pec50_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for pec50
columns.pec50_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for pec50
columns.emax_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for emax_estimate
columns.emax_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for emax_estimate
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_ci_lower: Lower bound of the 95% CI for emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate
columns.emax_vs_pos_ctrl_ci_upper: Upper bound of the 95% CI for emax_vs_pos_ctrl_estimate

pxr_single_concentration_train.csv.description: Single-concentration primary-screen training data (log2 fold-change) — a large, lower-resolution screen useful for pretraining or as auxiliary signal before fine-tuning on the dose-response pEC50 set.

columns.ocnt_batch: Octant batch identifier for the assayed material
columns.log2_fc_estimate: Induction signal as log2 fold-change vs control
columns.t_statistic: t-statistic for the fold-change vs control
columns.cohens_d: Cohen's d effect size for the induction response

pxr_96_compound_semi_pure_train.csv.description: Supplementary 96-compound 'uscale' semi-pure training set, released during Phase 2 to augment the primary training data with additional measured compounds. Includes Charged Aerosol Detector (CAD) analytical columns used to correct potency by the actual measured compound concentration.

columns.semi_pure_batch_id: Batch identifier for the semi-pure material
columns.semi_pure_ec50s: EC50(s) measured on the semi-pure material (M)
columns.semi_pure_pec50s: pEC50(s) measured on the semi-pure material
columns.semi_pure_emax_normalized: Emax normalized to the positive control
columns.corrected_semi_pure_ec50: EC50 corrected for measured compound concentration (M)
columns.semi_pure_theoretical_mass_on_column: Theoretical mass loaded on the LC column
columns.semi_pure_actual_mass_on_column: Actual mass on column from CAD quantitation
columns.semi_pure_correction_factor: Concentration correction factor applied to potency
columns.semi_pure_cad_peak_area_cv: Coefficient of variation of the CAD peak area
columns.semi_pure_cad_slope_cv: Coefficient of variation of the CAD calibration slope
columns.semi_pure_cad_yield_se: Standard error of the CAD-derived yield

pxr_htchem_libraries_train.csv.description: High-throughput chemistry (HT-chem) library screening data, released during Phase 2 — library-scale measurements that broaden training coverage of chemical space. Measured on crude library material, with Charged Aerosol Detector (CAD) analytical columns used to correct potency by the actual measured compound concentration.

columns.crude_batch_id: Batch identifier for the crude library material
columns.crude_ec50s: EC50(s) measured on the crude material (M)
columns.crude_pec50s: pEC50(s) measured on the crude material
columns.crude_emax_normalized: Emax normalized to the positive control
columns.corrected_crude_ec50: EC50 corrected for measured compound concentration (M)
columns.cad_yield_volatility_note: Note on CAD yield / volatility caveats for this compound
columns.crude_theoretical_mass_on_column: Theoretical mass loaded on the LC column
columns.crude_actual_mass_on_column: Actual mass on column from CAD quantitation
columns.crude_correction_factor: Concentration correction factor applied to potency
columns.crude_cad_peak_area_cv: Coefficient of variation of the CAD peak area
columns.crude_cad_slope_cv: Coefficient of variation of the CAD calibration slope
columns.crude_cad_yield_se: Standard error of the CAD-derived yield

pxr_structure_test_blinded.csv.description: Structure-track blinded test set: fragment-sized molecules for predicting protein-ligand complexes against PXR, scored by LDDT-PLI. Crystallographic data generated with the Fraser lab at UCSF. Distinct from the activity track.

abalone.csv.description: UCI Abalone dataset — physical measurements of abalone used to predict age (number of rings). A small, non-chemistry reference dataset for Workbench test pipelines.

columns.Class_number_of_rings: Rings + 1.5 gives age in years (prediction target)

test_data.csv.description: Synthetic person data (100 rows, seeded) for Workbench test pipelines — a fixed fixture with correlated numeric, categorical, boolean, and datetime columns.
